title: Understand: anatomical evidence of evolution
description: Check your understanding of anatomical evidence of evolution in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is an anatomical feature that could provide evidence of an evolutionary relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a body structure shared by two species
a DNA sequence shared by two species
a behavior shared by two species

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is an anatomical feature that could provide evidence of an evolutionary relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of these is an anatomical feature that could provide evidence of an evolutionary relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an organ system shared by two species
a protein amino acid sequence shared by two species
a mating behavior shared by two species

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best phrase to fill in the blank.**

>Shared features inherited from a common ancestor are called \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Choose the best phrase to fill in the blank.**

>Similar features that have evolved along separate evolutionary paths are called \_\_\_\_\_\_. 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: shared features inherited from a common ancestor
similar features that have evolved along separate evolutionary paths

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best word to fill in the blank.**

>Species that share more homologous features are \_\_\_\_\_\_ closely related.

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Choose the best word to fill in the blank.**

>Species that share fewer homologous features are \_\_\_\_\_\_ closely related.

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Choose the best word to fill in the blank.**

>Species that are more closely related share \_\_\_\_\_\_ homologous features.

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Determine if the statement below is true or false.**

>Scientists can compare the body structures of modern species, but not extinct species, to find evidence of evolution.

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Choose the best phrase to fill in the blank.**

> Scientists can compare the body structures of \_\_\_\_\_\_ to find anatomical evidence of evolution.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: modern species, but not extinct species

assessment_items.question_content:
**Determine if the statement below is true or false.**

>Scientists can compare the body structures of modern and extinct species to find evidence of evolution.

[[☃ radio 1]]
